# dependencytrack_policy_tag (Resource)

Manages the assignment of a tag to a policy in Dependency-Track. The tag must already exist (see the `dependencytrack_tag` resource).

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "dependencytrack_tag" "production" {
  name = "production"
}

resource "dependencytrack_policy" "license_policy" {
  name            = "License Compliance Policy"
  operator        = "ANY"
  violation_state = "FAIL"
}

# Limit the policy to projects tagged "production"
resource "dependencytrack_policy_tag" "license_policy_production" {
  tag    = dependencytrack_tag.production.name
  policy = dependencytrack_policy.license_policy.id
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `policy` (String) The UUID of the policy
- `tag` (String) The name of the tag. Dependency-Track normalizes tag names to lowercase, so a mixed-case name is matched case-insensitively (using a lowercase name is recommended). Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of the policy tag assignment in the format `tag_name/policy_uuid`

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Policy tag assignments can be imported using the format: tag_name/policy_uuid
terraform import dependencytrack_policy_tag.example production/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001
```
